import io
import string
import list

fn parse_line(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut i = 0
  mut in_num = false
  mut cur = 0
  mut neg = false
  while i <= n
    mut b = 0 - 1
    if i < n
      match string.byte_at(s, i)
      when Some(x)
        b = x
      when None
        b = 0 - 1
      end
    end
    let is_ws = b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 13 or b == 10 or b < 0
    if is_ws
      if in_num
        mut v = cur
        if neg
          v = 0 - v
        end
        result = list.push(result, v)
        in_num = false
        cur = 0
        neg = false
      end
    else
      if b == 45 and not in_num
        neg = true
        in_num = true
      else
        cur = cur * 10 + (b - 48)
        in_num = true
      end
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  result
end

fn parse_target(_ s: String) -> Int
  let trimmed = string.trim(s)
  match string.parse_int(trimmed)
  when Some(n)
    n
  when None
    0
  end
end

fn get_at(_ xs: List<Int>, _ i: Int) -> Int
  match xs.get(i)
  when Some(x)
    x
  when None
    0
  end
end

let l1 = io.read_line()
let l2 = io.read_line()

mut nums: List<Int> = []
match l1
when Some(s)
  nums = parse_line(s)
when None
  nums = []
end

mut t = 0
match l2
when Some(s)
  t = parse_target(s)
when None
  t = 0
end

let count = nums.len()
mut fi = 0 - 1
mut fj = 0 - 1
mut i = 0
while i < count
  mut j = i + 1
  while j < count
    if fi < 0
      let a = get_at(nums, i)
      let b = get_at(nums, j)
      if a + b == t
        fi = i
        fj = j
      end
    end
    j = j + 1
  end
  i = i + 1
end

if fi < 0
  print("none")
else
  print("found=#{fi},#{fj}")
end
